name: Chadian Arabic
notes: Chadian Arabic (ʕarabī šuwā). Spoken in Chad, northern Cameroon, CAR, and northeastern Nigeria. Arrived via Sudan/Saharan trade routes (~11th–14th c. CE). CONSERVATIVE: θ (ث) and ð (ذ) often preserved. ق → /q/ maintained. ض → /dˤ/ (lateral lost). Sub-Saharan Chadic substrate (Hausa, Kanuri, Sara, Maba, Fulani): implosive allophones /ɓ, ɗ/; /ŋ/ phoneme; prenasalised stops [ᵐb, ⁿd] in some varieties. Moderate vowel reduction.
